query: CHP olağanüstü kurultay çağrı heyeti Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi başvuru karar - latest status as of late June / early July 2026. Has Özgür Özel's team filed the application under Civil Code Art 75 requesting appointment of a 3-person çağrı heyeti to convene a CHP extraordinary congress? Has any court issued a decision?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the findings on the CHP olağanüstü kurultay çağrı heyeti Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi situation as of late June/early July 2026:

**Application status:** Özgür Özel's faction **announced and prepared** the application to the Ankara Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi requesting appointment of a çağrı heyeti (call committee) to convene the extraordinary congress. Aydın MP Süleyman Bülbül stated they would file "on Monday" during the week of ~June 29, 2026, after headquarters failed to act on the 833 delegate signatures submitted June 17.

**Court decision:** **No sourced confirmation of a final Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi decision** (grant/reject) on the çağrı heyeti request appears in the available reporting. The court was expected to decide within 1–3 months. A separate related application (by Tülin Apak) was rejected on procedural/standing grounds only.

**Legal basis nuance:** Turkish coverage foregrounded CHP bylaw **Article 48** (45-day rule) rather than explicitly naming **Medeni Kanun Art. 75** — which is the applicable statutory mechanism for a court-appointed call committee. The "3-person" composition was not verbatim confirmed in the retrieved coverage.

**Key context:** The crisis stems from the May 21, 2026 "mutlak butlan" ruling; July 20, 2026 (start of judicial recess and an expected Yargıtay ruling) is a critical marker in the litigation.

Note: AskNews live-news tools were unavailable due to account credit limits, so this relied on Turkish news web search and article fetches.

sources.snippet: Özel expected to first apply to the sulh hukuk mahkemesi requesting a call committee; July 20, 2026 Yargıtay decision and judicial recess referenced.

sources.snippet: Faction links political calculus and possible new formation to July 21, 2026.

key_facts: Özgür Özel's faction announced/prepared an application to the Ankara Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi (Civil Court of Peace) to request appointment of a çağrı heyeti (call committee) to convene a CHP extraordinary congress; filing was stated as 'Monday' the week of ~June 29, 2026.

833 delegate signatures (some reports ~830) plus İstanbul delegates' will-statements were submitted to CHP headquarters on June 17, 2026, with a ~8–10 day deadline for the leadership to call a congress.
The deadline passed with no congress call from the interim (Kılıçdaroğlu-linked) management, triggering the decision to seek a judicial remedy (call committee).
Aydın MP Süleyman Bülbül publicly stated they would apply to the civil court and request a call committee, citing bylaw Article 48's 45-day rule.
The crisis stems from the Ankara Bölge Adliye Mahkemesi 36th Civil Chamber's May 21, 2026 'mutlak butlan' (absolute nullity) decision that terminated Özel and party organs' duties.

The applicable statutory basis for a court-appointed call committee on member/delegate request is Turkish Civil Code (Medeni Kanun) Art. 75; Turkish coverage emphasized bylaw Art. 48 rather than naming 'TMK 75' explicitly.

No sourced confirmation that the Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi issued a final decision (accept/reject) on the çağrı heyeti request as of the latest available reporting (~July 9, 2026); a decision was expected within 1–3 months.
If granted, an extraordinary congress would be expected within 45 days of the court's decision.
A related application by Tülin Apak was rejected on procedural/standing grounds (lacked taraf sıfatı), not on the merits of the çağrı heyeti request.
July 20, 2026 is significant as the start of judicial recess and the expected deadline for a Yargıtay ruling on objections to a precautionary measure in the broader congress litigation.
İstinaf (regional appellate) had earlier rejected an extraordinary congress request in the broader saga, per Yeni Şafak.
Özel's faction linked its political calculus (possible new formation with ~100 MPs, '21 Temmuz') to developments around July 20–21, 2026.

data_points.metric: Delegate signatures collected for extraordinary congress request
Bylaw Art. 48 timeline to hold extraordinary congress after signature submission
Expected time for Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi to decide on the çağrı heyeti application (upper bound)
MPs cited for potential new political formation post-July 21
